    • NEB Method Comparison

Fit a Bayesian benchmark model with a custom formula

Source: R/models.R
bc_fit_design.Rd

Full brms formula passthrough for factorial designs, interactions, and custom random effects. Use when bc_fit() is too restrictive (e.g., algorithm * rotation_removal interactions or (1 | mol_id:spin)).

Usage

bc_fit_design(
  data,
  formula,
  family = NULL,
  prior = NULL,
  cens_col = NULL,
  diagnostics = TRUE,
  chains = 4,
  iter = 4000,
  warmup = 1000,
  cores = 4,
  seed = 1995,
  backend = NULL,
  file = NULL,
  adapt_delta = 0.99,
  max_treedepth = 12,
  ...
)

Arguments

data

Long-format benchmark data.

formula

A brms::bf() formula object.

family

A brms family object. If NULL, auto-detected from the response variable in the formula.

prior

A brmsprior object. If NULL, uses bc_default_priors().

cens_col

Name of a binary censoring column (optional).

diagnostics

Logical. Run convergence diagnostics after fitting?

chains, iter, warmup, cores, seed, backend, file, adapt_delta, max_treedepth

Passed to brms::brm().

...

Additional arguments passed to brms::brm().

Value

A brmsfit object.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
data <- bc_simulate_benchmark(n_systems = 20)
formula <- brms::bf(count ~ method + (1 | system_id))
model <- bc_fit_design(data, formula = formula)
} # }
